title: LED Light Box Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale LED light box signs with high-efficiency edge-lit or back-lit systems. Uniform illumination, slim profiles, UL 48 listed. 3-week German-engineered delivery.
heroSubtitle: High-efficiency LED illumination in sleek, slim-profile light box cabinets. Uniform brightness, low energy consumption, and effortless graphic changes.

sections.heading: LED Technology Transforms Light Box Signage
sections.content:
LED light box signs have replaced fluorescent-lit cabinets as the standard in illuminated commercial signage, and the advantages are compelling across every metric that matters. Energy consumption drops by 50-70%, lamp life extends from 20,000 hours to 50,000+, and light uniformity improves dramatically thanks to precise LED module spacing and optical diffusion engineering.

Sunlite Signs manufactures LED light box signs in both edge-lit and direct-lit (back-lit) configurations, each optimized for different size ranges and brightness requirements. Our edge-lit systems produce remarkably thin profiles — as slim as 1" deep — for applications where a sleek, modern aesthetic is essential. Our direct-lit systems deliver higher brightness levels for larger signs where maximum luminance is needed.

Every LED light box sign we produce carries UL 48 certification and is built with Samsung or Nichia LED modules paired with UL-recognized Mean Well power supplies. Our German engineering approach treats the light box as an integrated system — not a collection of parts — ensuring that the LEDs, optics, power supply, and cabinet work together for optimal performance and longevity.

sections.heading: Edge-Lit vs. Direct-Lit: Choosing the Right System
sections.content:
Understanding the difference between edge-lit and direct-lit LED light boxes helps you spec the right product for each project:

  • Edge-Lit Light Boxes: LEDs are mounted along the edges of the cabinet, and an acrylic light guide panel distributes light uniformly across the sign face. Produces ultra-slim cabinets (1" to 2" deep) ideal for indoor retail, menu boards, and wall-mounted displays. Best for signs up to approximately 48" x 72".
  • Direct-Lit Light Boxes: LED modules are mounted across the back panel of the cabinet, with a diffusion layer between the LEDs and the sign face to ensure even illumination. Produces brighter output suitable for larger signs and outdoor applications. Cabinet depth is typically 4" to 6".

For most indoor retail and commercial applications, edge-lit light boxes are the preferred choice due to their slim profile and elegant appearance. For outdoor monument signs, large-format wall signs, and applications requiring high brightness, direct-lit systems deliver superior performance.

Sunlite Signs can recommend the optimal system for any project based on the sign dimensions, viewing distance, ambient light conditions, and mounting constraints. Send us your project specifications and we'll provide a detailed recommendation with supporting technical data.

sections.heading: Graphic Flexibility & Changeability
sections.content:
One of the most valuable features of LED light box signs is the ability to change graphics quickly and economically. Unlike channel letters or monument signs where the messaging is permanently built into the structure, light box graphics can be swapped in minutes — making them ideal for businesses that update their messaging seasonally, promote rotating products, or need to reflect brand refreshes without replacing the entire sign.

Sunlite LED light boxes accept graphics in these formats:

  • SEG Fabric: Dye-sublimation printed fabric with a silicone edge gasket that presses into an aluminum extrusion frame. Produces perfectly tensioned, wrinkle-free graphics with vibrant backlit color rendition.
  • Backlit Film: Translucent inkjet-printed film mounted behind a clear or frosted acrylic face. Cost-effective for smaller light boxes and menu board applications.
  • Duratrans: High-resolution photographic translucency for applications requiring photographic image quality, such as luxury retail and hospitality.

We design every light box with tool-free graphic change capability, so your client's staff can swap graphics without calling a sign company. For SEG systems, the fabric simply pulls out and the new graphic pushes in. For film-based systems, the face snaps open for quick access to the graphic layer. This self-service capability is a strong selling point that differentiates light boxes from permanent signage.

faqs.question: How thin can an LED light box sign be?
faqs.answer: Our edge-lit light box signs can be as thin as 1" deep while maintaining uniform illumination across the sign face. This slim profile makes them ideal for indoor retail environments where a sleek, modern look is desired.

faqs.question: How often do LED modules need to be replaced?
faqs.answer: Our Samsung and Nichia LED modules are rated for 50,000+ hours to L70 — approximately 11 years of dusk-to-dawn operation. In practice, most LED light boxes will never need module replacement during their useful service life.

faqs.question: Can clients change the graphics themselves?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our light boxes are designed for tool-free graphic changes. SEG fabric graphics pull out and push in. Film-based systems use snap-open frames for easy access. We provide graphic change instructions with every unit.

faqs.question: Are LED light box signs suitable for outdoor use?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our direct-lit light boxes are engineered for outdoor installation with sealed cabinets, weather-resistant materials, and IP65-rated LED modules. Edge-lit models are primarily designed for indoor use but can be adapted for covered outdoor locations.

faqs.question: What is the energy savings compared to fluorescent light boxes?
faqs.answer: LED light boxes typically consume 50-70% less electricity than equivalent fluorescent-lit cabinets. A 4' x 8' LED light box might draw 150-200 watts compared to 400-600 watts for a fluorescent unit of the same size.

title: SEG Fabric Light Boxes | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale SEG fabric light boxes with precision aluminum extrusion frames. Wrinkle-free tensioned graphics, tool-free changes, UL 48 listed. 3-week trade delivery.
heroSubtitle: Precision-extruded aluminum frames with silicone edge gasket fabric graphics. Perfectly tensioned, seamlessly illuminated, and effortlessly changeable.

sections.heading: The SEG Advantage in Illuminated Signage
sections.content:
SEG (Silicone Edge Graphics) technology has rapidly become the gold standard for light box signage, and for compelling reasons. The system uses a silicone gasket sewn to the perimeter of a dye-sublimation printed fabric that presses into a precision-machined channel in an aluminum extrusion frame. The result is a perfectly tensioned, wrinkle-free graphic surface that stretches seamlessly across the light box face with no visible frame edges or fasteners.

The visual difference between an SEG light box and a traditional film-behind-acrylic light box is immediately apparent. SEG graphics have a soft, fabric texture that looks premium and contemporary. The tensioning system eliminates the sagging, bubbling, and wrinkling that plague film-based systems over time. And because the fabric is front-loaded from the face of the frame, graphic changes take minutes without tools or specialized skills.

Sunlite Signs manufactures SEG light box frames from our proprietary aluminum extrusion profiles, which are designed for optimal fabric tensioning, LED integration, and structural rigidity. Our German engineering heritage is evident in every detail — from the precision of the gasket channel to the quality of the corner connections.

sections.heading: Frame Construction & LED Integration
sections.content:
Our SEG light box frames are built to perform as integrated illumination systems, not just graphic holders with lights bolted inside. Here's what goes into every Sunlite SEG light box:

  • Extrusion Profile: Proprietary 6063-T5 aluminum extrusion with integrated gasket channel, LED mounting rail, and structural ribbing. Available in multiple depths for edge-lit and direct-lit configurations.
  • Corner Connections: Precision-machined aluminum corner keys that provide rigid 90-degree joints without visible fasteners. Corners are the weak point in many competitors' frames — ours are engineered for long-term structural integrity.
  • LED System: Edge-lit models use high-output LED strip arrays mounted along the frame interior with an acrylic light guide panel for uniform diffusion. Direct-lit models use matrix-mounted LED modules with a separate diffusion layer.
  • Power Supply: UL-recognized Mean Well drivers concealed within the frame or in an external junction box, depending on frame depth and configuration.

Every frame is assembled, squared, and tested in our facility before shipping. We verify illumination uniformity across the entire face and check fabric tension with a test graphic to ensure proper fit. Frames that don't meet our standards get reworked — we don't ship marginal product.

sections.heading: Fabric Graphic Production & Quality
sections.content:
The graphic is half the system, and graphic quality is just as important as frame quality. Sunlite Signs produces SEG fabric graphics using commercial dye-sublimation printing for wholesale trade orders on premium 240gsm backlit display fabric, optimized for our LED module systems:

  • Print Resolution: Up to 1200 DPI for photographic-quality reproduction of images, logos, and fine typography
  • Color Gamut: Wide-gamut dye-sublimation inks produce vibrant, saturated colors that look spectacular when backlit by our LED system
  • Fabric Quality: Premium polyester display fabric with consistent opacity and light transmission. Every roll is inspected for weave defects before printing.
  • Gasket Installation: Medical-grade silicone gasket is sewn to the fabric perimeter using a CNC-guided sewing system for consistent gasket positioning and tension

We can print from your client's artwork files (high-resolution PDF, TIFF, or PSD at 150+ DPI at full size) or work with your design team to optimize files for backlit fabric output. Color proofing is available for color-critical projects — we produce a printed swatch on the actual production fabric for your approval before the full graphic is printed.

Replacement graphics can be ordered independently from the frame, making it economical for clients to refresh their messaging seasonally or for promotions. Turnaround on replacement graphics is typically 5-7 business days.

faqs.question: What does SEG stand for?
faqs.answer: SEG stands for Silicone Edge Graphics. It refers to the system where a thin silicone gasket is sewn around the perimeter of a printed fabric graphic, and that gasket presses into a channel in the aluminum frame to create a perfectly tensioned, seamless display.

faqs.question: How difficult is it to change an SEG graphic?
faqs.answer: Very easy. The fabric graphic simply pulls out of the frame channel and the new graphic pushes in by hand. No tools are required. Most single-panel graphic changes take less than five minutes. We include graphic change instructions with every light box.

faqs.question: Can SEG light boxes be used outdoors?
faqs.answer: Our standard SEG frames are designed for indoor use. For outdoor applications, we offer a weather-sealed version with gasketed corners and UV-resistant fabric. However, fabric graphics will have a shorter outdoor lifespan than indoor, so budget for more frequent graphic replacements.

faqs.question: What size SEG light boxes can you produce?
faqs.answer: Our standard production handles SEG light boxes up to 10' x 8' as a single unit. Larger installations are achieved with multiple frames butted together — our extrusion profiles are designed to join seamlessly for continuous graphic walls.

faqs.question: How long do SEG fabric graphics last?
faqs.answer: Indoor SEG graphics maintain their color vibrancy for 3-5 years under normal conditions. In high-traffic retail environments where graphics are changed frequently for promotions, the frame will outlast dozens of graphic changes over its lifetime.

title: Slim Light Box Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale slim light box signs as thin as 1 inch. Edge-lit LED technology, uniform illumination, snap-open or SEG frames. German-engineered, 3-week trade delivery.
heroSubtitle: Ultra-thin illuminated sign cabinets as slim as one inch. Edge-lit LED technology delivers uniform brightness in a profile that hugs the wall.

sections.heading: Thin Is In: The Rise of Slim Light Box Signage
sections.content:
As commercial interiors trend toward clean lines and minimal visual clutter, slim light box signs have become the preferred format for illuminated messaging. Traditional deep-cabinet light boxes looked bulky and dated by comparison — slim light boxes blend into the architecture rather than dominating it, making them appropriate for sophisticated retail, hospitality, and corporate environments.

Sunlite Signs manufactures slim light boxes using edge-lit LED technology that delivers remarkably uniform illumination from a cabinet depth of just 1" to 2". Our light guide panel (LGP) technology uses a precision-etched acrylic panel to distribute edge-mounted LED light evenly across the entire sign face, eliminating the hot spots and dark areas that plagued early edge-lit designs.

The slim profile doesn't come at the expense of build quality. Our aluminum extrusion frames are engineered for rigidity and durability, with the same German precision we apply to every product in our line. The LEDs are long-life Samsung modules, the power supplies are UL-recognized Mean Well units, and every assembled light box carries UL 48 certification.

sections.heading: Applications Where Slim Profile Matters
sections.content:
Slim light box signs shine in environments where depth matters — both aesthetically and practically:

  • Retail Storefronts: Window-mounted slim light boxes that don't block sightlines into the store while providing bright, attractive signage visible from the sidewalk
  • Corridor & Hallway Signs: Wall-mounted light boxes in narrow hallways where deep cabinets would reduce pedestrian clearance or violate ADA corridor width requirements
  • Menu Boards: Slim illuminated menu displays above service counters in restaurants and cafes, where depth must be minimal to avoid head-clearance issues
  • Real Estate Displays: Property listing displays in real estate offices where multiple slim light boxes are arranged side by side for a clean, gallery-like presentation
  • Trade Show & Exhibit: Portable slim light boxes that pack flat for shipping and assemble quickly at event venues

Our slim light boxes are available in snap-open frame and SEG fabric frame configurations. Snap-open frames use a hinged aluminum perimeter that springs open to reveal the graphic area — ideal for frequent poster or print changes. SEG frames use silicone-edged fabric graphics for a seamless, premium appearance.

sections.heading: Technical Specifications & Customization
sections.content:
Standard specifications for Sunlite slim light box signs:

  • Cabinet Depth: 1" (25mm) for snap-open frames, 1.5" (38mm) for SEG frames, 2" (50mm) for deep-edge-lit models
  • Frame Material: Anodized 6063-T5 aluminum extrusion in silver, black, or white as standard. Custom anodize colors and powder-coat colors available.
  • LED System: Samsung 2835 edge-lit strips with LGP (light guide panel) diffusion, 4000K standard CCT, 6500K and 3000K available
  • Power: Mean Well plug-in or hardwired drivers, 100-277V input, UL recognized
  • Graphic Area: Standard sizes from 18" x 24" to 48" x 72". Custom sizes to any specification.

Customization options include rounded corners, continuous LED dimming, occupancy sensor integration, and multi-panel ganged configurations for graphic walls. We also offer single-sided and double-sided models — double-sided slim light boxes are popular for ceiling-hung wayfinding applications where the sign needs to be readable from both approach directions.

Every slim light box is fully assembled and tested before shipping. We include the power supply, mounting hardware, and a graphic template showing the exact printable area with bleed and safety margins. Lead time is three weeks from approved specifications.

faqs.question: How uniform is the illumination on a 1-inch-deep light box?
faqs.answer: Our light guide panel technology produces illumination uniformity of 85% or better across the sign face, even at 1" depth. This is comparable to the uniformity of direct-lit cabinets that are 4-6" deep, achieved through precision optical engineering of the LGP extraction pattern.

faqs.question: Can slim light boxes be ceiling-mounted?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer ceiling-mount kits with aircraft cables or rigid rod suspension for slim light boxes. Double-sided models are especially popular for ceiling-hung wayfinding applications in retail and hospitality environments.

faqs.question: Are slim light boxes available in double-sided?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our double-sided slim light boxes use back-to-back edge-lit LGP panels in a single frame, typically at 2" total depth. Both sides are independently illuminated for consistent brightness regardless of which side you're viewing.

faqs.question: What is the maximum size for an edge-lit slim light box?
faqs.answer: Edge-lit technology works best up to approximately 48" x 72" in a single panel. Beyond that size, we recommend our direct-lit system or a multi-panel edge-lit configuration to maintain illumination uniformity.

title: Double-Sided Light Boxes | Wholesale | Sunlite
metaDescription: Wholesale double-sided light boxes for ceiling-hung and freestanding displays. Uniform LED illumination on both faces. German-engineered, 3-week sign trade delivery.
heroSubtitle: Dual-face illuminated displays visible from both directions. Ideal for ceiling-hung wayfinding, freestanding kiosks, and window-mounted signage.

sections.heading: Two-Sided Visibility for Maximum Impact
sections.content:
Double-sided light boxes deliver illuminated messaging to viewers approaching from either direction, making them indispensable for environments where foot traffic flows in both directions. Ceiling-hung double-sided light boxes are the workhorses of retail wayfinding, guiding shoppers through department stores, malls, airports, and large commercial spaces with bright, visible overhead signage.

Sunlite Signs manufactures double-sided light boxes in both edge-lit and direct-lit configurations, with each face independently illuminated for consistent brightness. Our engineering ensures that neither face is compromised — both sides receive equal LED coverage and diffusion, so the sign looks equally professional regardless of the viewing direction.

The applications extend well beyond ceiling-hung wayfinding. Window-mounted double-sided light boxes display messaging to both interior and exterior viewers simultaneously. Freestanding floor units serve as directory signs and promotional displays in lobbies and atriums. And wall-mounted projecting models function as illuminated blade signs for corridor and storefront identification.

sections.heading: Mounting Configurations & Hardware
sections.content:
We offer double-sided light boxes with multiple mounting systems to suit different installation scenarios:

  • Ceiling Suspension: Aircraft cable kits with adjustable height, or rigid rod systems for fixed heights. Both options include ceiling anchors and turnbuckles for precise leveling. Weight-rated for the specific light box size.
  • Freestanding Floor Mount: Weighted base or bolt-down pedestal for lobby and atrium installations. Available in single-post or dual-post configurations depending on the light box dimensions.
  • Wall Projecting: Bracket-mounted to project perpendicular from the wall face, functioning as an illuminated blade sign. Brackets include concealed conduit routing for clean power wire management.
  • Window Mount: Suction-cup or through-glass mounting systems for window displays that face both inward and outward.

Every mounting system is engineered for the specific weight of the light box and the dynamic loads it will experience in service (primarily air movement from HVAC systems for ceiling-hung units). We provide complete mounting hardware, installation instructions, and load-rated specifications with every order.

Power routing is an important consideration for ceiling-hung units. We offer cord-and-plug configurations for applications near existing outlets, and hardwire configurations for clean installations where the power is routed through the ceiling structure. Our wiring exits through the suspension hardware for a completely concealed power connection.

faqs.question: Are both sides of the light box equally bright?
faqs.answer: Yes. Each face has its own independent LED system and diffusion layer, ensuring identical brightness on both sides. We test every double-sided unit for face-to-face brightness consistency before shipping.

faqs.question: How much do ceiling-hung double-sided light boxes weigh?
faqs.answer: Weight varies by size. A typical 24" x 36" double-sided edge-lit unit weighs approximately 12-15 lbs. A 48" x 72" direct-lit unit weighs approximately 35-45 lbs. We provide exact weights for all suspension hardware calculations.

faqs.question: Can each side display a different graphic?
faqs.answer: Yes. Each face accepts its own independent graphic, so you can display different content on each side. This is useful for directional wayfinding where each face points toward different destinations.

faqs.question: What suspension cable length is available?
faqs.answer: Our standard aircraft cable kits include cables up to 10 feet long, adjustable to any shorter length. For installations requiring longer suspension, custom cable lengths are available on request.

faqs.question: Are double-sided light boxes available in SEG fabric format?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer double-sided SEG light boxes where both faces use silicone-edge fabric graphics. The frame accepts independent fabric panels on each side, allowing for quick, tool-free graphic changes on either face.

title: Retail Light Box Displays | Wholesale | Sunlite
metaDescription: Wholesale retail light box displays for storefronts, window displays, and in-store merchandising. Easy graphic changes, UL 48 listed. 3-week German-engineered delivery.
heroSubtitle: Backlit displays that make products and promotions impossible to ignore. Quick-change graphics for seasonal messaging, campaign rollouts, and everyday brand storytelling.

sections.heading: Light Box Displays as Retail Revenue Drivers
sections.content:
In retail environments, illuminated displays outperform non-lit signage by a wide margin. Studies show that backlit graphics capture 2-3 times more visual attention than static, unlit signs — translating directly to increased product awareness, stronger promotional response, and higher per-visit sales. For your retail clients, light box displays aren't a luxury; they're a proven revenue driver.

Sunlite Signs manufactures retail light box displays designed for the unique demands of the retail environment: frequent graphic changes, high visual impact, slim profiles that don't encroach on valuable selling floor space, and construction that holds up to the daily wear of busy commercial spaces. Our light boxes are tools that work as hard as the retail staff.

Our wholesale pricing structure allows sign shops to equip entire store environments with professional-grade light box displays while maintaining margins that make retail projects worthwhile. Whether the project calls for a single feature display or a full-store graphic program with 50+ light boxes, Sunlite delivers consistent quality and competitive pricing.

sections.heading: Retail-Optimized Design Features
sections.content:
Retail light boxes face different demands than corporate or wayfinding applications. Sunlite Signs addresses these requirements with features designed specifically for retail environments:

  • Quick-Change Graphics: Snap-open frames or SEG fabric systems that allow store staff to swap graphics in minutes without tools. This enables seasonal refreshes, promotional changeovers, and campaign rollouts without scheduling a sign crew visit.
  • Modular Sizing: Standard sizes designed to work together in grid configurations for graphic walls. Our most popular retail sizes (24" x 36", 36" x 48", and 48" x 72") share common mounting hardware for interchangeable installations.
  • Daisy-Chain Power: Light boxes can be connected in series from a single power source, reducing the number of electrical connections needed for multi-unit installations.
  • Locking Options: Tamper-resistant hardware options for light boxes in public areas where unauthorized graphic changes or theft are concerns.

Our retail light boxes are also designed for easy maintenance. LED modules are accessible from the back panel without removing the unit from the wall. Power supplies are plug-in replaceable. And the frames themselves are finished with a durable anodized coating that resists scratches and fingerprints in high-traffic retail environments.

sections.heading: Retail Graphic Programs & Volume Production
sections.content:
National and regional retail brands often need large quantities of light boxes deployed across multiple store locations simultaneously. Sunlite Signs supports these rollout programs with scalable production capacity and program management capabilities:

  • Volume Production: Our manufacturing facility can produce hundreds of light box units per month with consistent quality across every unit in the run
  • Kit Packing: Light boxes can be packed in store-specific kits with all hardware, graphics, and installation instructions for each location
  • Drop Shipping: We can ship directly to individual store locations or to a central distribution point, depending on your logistics preference
  • Graphic Templates: We provide graphic templates with exact bleed, trim, and safety margins for each light box size, ensuring your client's design team or printer can produce correctly sized graphics

For sign shops that manage retail accounts, Sunlite is the production partner that allows you to say yes to large-scale programs without investing in light box manufacturing capacity. We handle the production; you handle the client relationship and installation. Standard lead time for volume programs is three to four weeks from approved specifications, depending on quantity.

faqs.question: How often can retail light box graphics be changed?
faqs.answer: There's no limit. Our snap-open and SEG frame systems are designed for unlimited graphic changes. Many retail clients change graphics monthly or seasonally. The frame is a long-term investment; graphics are the consumable element.

faqs.question: Can retail light boxes be daisy-chained together?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our retail light boxes include daisy-chain power connectors that allow multiple units to share a single electrical circuit. This simplifies installation significantly when deploying multiple light boxes in a single location.

faqs.question: Do you offer tamper-resistant light box options?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer security screw options for snap-open frames and locking SEG frames that require a key tool to open. These are recommended for light boxes in public areas or unmonitored spaces.

faqs.question: What is the minimum order quantity for a retail rollout?
faqs.answer: There is no minimum order quantity. We produce single units and large production runs with equal attention to quality. Volume pricing applies to orders of 10 or more identical units, with additional discounts for larger quantities.

title: Menu Board Light Boxes | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale menu board light boxes with LED illumination and easy graphic changes. Perfect for restaurants, cafes, and QSR. German-engineered, 3-week trade delivery.
heroSubtitle: Bright, professional menu displays that make food look irresistible and pricing easy to read. Slim profiles, uniform LED lighting, and quick graphic changes.

sections.heading: Menu Board Light Boxes That Sell Food
sections.content:
In the food service industry, the menu board is the single most important piece of in-store signage. It's where purchasing decisions are made — often in seconds — and the quality of the menu display directly influences what and how much customers order. Backlit menu boards increase average order value by 15-30% compared to non-illuminated boards, because the lighting makes food photography look more appetizing and makes pricing and item descriptions easier to read.

Sunlite Signs manufactures menu board light boxes optimized for the food service environment. Our slim-profile edge-lit cabinets mount flush to the wall above service counters, delivering bright, even illumination without the visual bulk of traditional deep-cabinet menu boards. The result is a cleaner, more modern look that appeals to today's restaurant brands.

Our menu board light boxes are designed for the reality of restaurant operations: graphics need to change when menu items or prices change, the sign needs to handle the heat and humidity of a kitchen-adjacent environment, and it needs to look clean and professional every day. We build all of these considerations into the product from the design stage.

sections.heading: Configuration Options for Food Service
sections.content:
Restaurant menu displays come in many configurations, and Sunlite Signs produces all of them:

  • Single-Panel Menu Boards: One large light box displaying the complete menu. Popular for small cafes, juice bars, and specialty food shops with focused menus.
  • Multi-Panel Arrays: Three, four, or five light boxes mounted side by side to create a continuous menu wall. Each panel can be independently updated, allowing partial menu changes without reprinting the entire display.
  • Topper Boards: A horizontal light box mounted above the main menu panels, typically displaying the restaurant brand, logo, or featured promotions.
  • Drive-Through Boards: Weatherproof outdoor menu light boxes engineered for drive-through lane applications, with anti-glare faces and high-brightness LED systems for daylight readability.

Our multi-panel menu systems use a continuous mounting rail that aligns all panels perfectly and conceals the seams between adjacent units. The result looks like a single, seamless display even though it's composed of individually changeable panels. This modular approach gives restaurants the flexibility to update individual sections of the menu without the cost of reprinting the entire display.

sections.heading: Durability in Kitchen-Adjacent Environments
sections.content:
Menu board light boxes operate in challenging conditions. Mounted above service counters, they're exposed to rising heat, steam, cooking grease vapor, and frequent cleaning with commercial kitchen chemicals. A light box that can't handle these conditions won't last.

Sunlite menu board light boxes are built with these environmental factors in mind:

  • Sealed Cabinet: Gasketed construction prevents grease vapor and moisture from reaching the LED system and electrical components
  • Easy-Clean Surfaces: Anodized aluminum frames and smooth acrylic or fabric faces that can be wiped down with standard kitchen cleaning solutions without damage
  • Heat-Rated Components: LED modules and power supplies rated for ambient temperatures up to 50°C (122°F), well above typical above-counter conditions
  • Grease-Resistant Finish: Optional clear-coat treatment that prevents grease buildup on the frame surface and simplifies regular cleaning

For drive-through applications, our outdoor menu board light boxes add weatherproof construction, anti-condensation features, and high-brightness LED systems that remain readable in direct sunlight. These outdoor units are UL 48 listed for wet locations and carry a structural warranty against weather-related failure.

faqs.question: How easy is it to update menu graphics?
faqs.answer: Very easy. Our snap-open frame menu boards allow graphic changes in under two minutes per panel. SEG fabric menu boards are equally quick. Restaurant staff can update menu panels themselves without calling a sign company.

faqs.question: Are menu board light boxes suitable for drive-through lanes?
faqs.answer: Yes. We produce outdoor-rated menu board light boxes specifically for drive-through applications. These units feature sealed weatherproof cabinets, high-brightness LED systems for daylight readability, and anti-glare face panels.

faqs.question: Can grease vapor damage the LED system?
faqs.answer: Not in our menu board light boxes. The cabinet is sealed with gaskets to prevent grease vapor and moisture from reaching the internal LED modules and power supply. The sealed design also prevents insects from entering the light box.

faqs.question: What sizes are most common for restaurant menu boards?
faqs.answer: The most popular single-panel size is 24" x 36" in portrait orientation. Multi-panel arrays typically use 22" x 28" or 24" x 36" panels side by side. Drive-through menu boards range from 4' x 4' to 4' x 8' depending on the menu complexity.

title: Real Estate Light Boxes | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale real estate light boxes for property listing displays. Slim LED-lit panels, easy listing changes, cable or wall-mount. German-engineered, 3-week trade delivery.
heroSubtitle: Elegant backlit property listing displays that showcase listings 24/7. Slim profiles, quick-change graphics, and cable-hung or wall-mounted configurations.

sections.heading: Window Displays That Sell Properties Around the Clock
sections.content:
Real estate offices depend on their window displays to generate leads from walk-by traffic, and illuminated light box displays outperform printed listings by every measure. Backlit property photos look dramatically more appealing than printed sheets taped to a window, and the illumination makes listings visible and attractive after dark when potential buyers are walking or driving through the neighborhood.

Sunlite Signs manufactures real estate light box systems designed specifically for property listing displays. Our cable-hung and wall-mounted light box panels create a clean, gallery-like presentation that elevates the real estate brand while making individual listings the focal point. The systems are designed for frequent listing changes — because in a busy real estate office, listings change weekly or even daily.

Our wholesale pricing makes it feasible for sign shops to equip real estate offices with professional-grade light box systems that compete with the digital display installations that larger agencies use. For a fraction of the cost of digital screens, our light box systems deliver comparable visual impact with the added benefit of zero ongoing content management costs.

sections.heading: Cable-Hung & Wall-Mount Display Systems
sections.content:
We offer two primary configuration approaches for real estate light box displays:

  • Cable-Hung Systems: Individual light box panels suspended on stainless steel cables that run from ceiling to floor or across a window frame. Panels can be arranged in columns and rows at adjustable heights. The cable system creates a floating, contemporary aesthetic that's become the signature look for upscale real estate offices.
  • Wall-Mounted Systems: Light box panels mounted directly to the wall in grid arrangements. Cleaner and more permanent than cable systems, ideal for offices where the display configuration won't change.

Cable-hung systems use our slim edge-lit light box panels — typically 1" to 1.5" deep — in standard sizes that correspond to common listing card dimensions. The most popular configuration is a grid of portrait-oriented panels in either A3 (11.7" x 16.5") or A4 (8.3" x 11.7") format, though we produce custom sizes to match any listing card template.

Each panel accepts a slide-in translucent graphic that can be printed on any standard inkjet printer using backlit film media. This means the real estate office can produce and change listing cards in-house without waiting for a print shop. New listing goes live in the morning, and it's in the window display by afternoon.

sections.heading: Creating a Complete Window Display Program
sections.content:
A successful real estate window display is more than individual light boxes — it's a coordinated visual program that presents the office's brand and listings in a cohesive, professional manner. Sunlite Signs helps sign shops deliver complete programs:

  • Brand Header Panel: A larger light box at the top of the display featuring the real estate company's logo and branding. This anchors the display and establishes brand identity.
  • Listing Panels: Individual light box units sized for property listing cards. Arranged in a grid below the brand header.
  • Feature Panel: One or two larger panels for premium listings or featured properties that deserve extra prominence in the display.
  • Cable System: Stainless steel suspension cables with all hardware, spacers, and leveling adjustments for a clean, professional installation.

We supply the complete system as a kit — all light box panels, cables, hardware, power supplies, and installation documentation packaged together for efficient on-site assembly. Our systems are designed so a two-person crew can install a full window display in under four hours, with no specialized tools required beyond a drill and level.

For sign shops looking to develop real estate as a vertical market, Sunlite's light box display systems are a compelling product category with strong repeat business potential. Every new listing is a graphic print opportunity, and every office renovation is a hardware upgrade opportunity.

faqs.question: Can real estate offices print their own listing cards?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our light box panels accept translucent backlit film that can be printed on standard wide-format inkjet printers. We provide graphic templates with correct dimensions, bleed areas, and color profiles. Many real estate offices produce all their listing graphics in-house.

faqs.question: How many light box panels fit in a standard storefront window?
faqs.answer: A typical 6-foot-wide storefront window comfortably accommodates 8-12 A3-size cable-hung panels in a 2-3 column by 3-4 row grid, plus a brand header panel. We can design custom configurations based on your client's window dimensions and listing volume.

faqs.question: Are cable-hung light boxes hard to install?
faqs.answer: No. Our cable systems use a straightforward tensioning mechanism that a two-person crew can install in under four hours for a full window display. We include complete hardware, templates, and step-by-step instructions with every system.

faqs.question: Do real estate light boxes use a lot of electricity?
faqs.answer: No. Our edge-lit LED panels are extremely energy-efficient. A typical 12-panel display consumes approximately 100-150 watts total — less than two standard light bulbs. Annual electricity cost is typically under $50 running 12 hours per day.

title: Edge-Lit Light Boxes | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale edge-lit light boxes using precision LGP technology for ultra-thin, uniform illumination. As slim as 1 inch deep. German-engineered, 3-week trade delivery.
heroSubtitle: Light guide panel technology delivers uniform LED illumination from an incredibly thin cabinet. The engineering behind the slimmest, most elegant light box signs available.

sections.heading: How Edge-Lit Technology Works
sections.content:
Edge-lit light boxes represent the most elegant approach to illuminated signage engineering. Instead of placing LED modules directly behind the sign face (as in traditional back-lit designs), edge-lit systems mount LED strips along the edges of a precision-engineered acrylic light guide panel (LGP). The LGP captures the edge-injected light and redirects it forward through a pattern of microscopic extraction dots etched into the back surface of the panel.

The result is remarkably uniform illumination from a cabinet that can be as slim as one inch deep. The technology eliminates the need for depth between the LEDs and the diffusion surface, which is what makes traditional back-lit light boxes four to six inches deep. For applications where slim profile matters — retail, hospitality, office, and residential environments — edge-lit technology is the clear winner.

Sunlite Signs has invested heavily in edge-lit LGP technology, and our light guide panels are manufactured using a proprietary dot-pattern algorithm that optimizes light extraction uniformity across the entire panel surface. Unlike commodity LGPs that simply use a standard dot gradient, our panels are custom-calculated for each size to account for the specific LED strip spacing, power, and color temperature being used. This attention to optical engineering is what produces the uniform, professional illumination our trade partners expect.

sections.heading: LGP Engineering & Optical Performance
sections.content:
The light guide panel is the heart of every edge-lit light box, and its engineering determines the quality of illumination. Here's what goes into a Sunlite LGP:

  • Material: Premium cast acrylic sheet (PMMA) with optical-grade clarity and consistent thickness. Cast acrylic provides superior light transmission compared to extruded alternatives.
  • Extraction Pattern: CNC-etched or silk-screened dot pattern calculated using our proprietary optical simulation software. Dot density increases toward the center of the panel (farther from the LED edges) to compensate for light attenuation.
  • Reflector: A high-efficiency white reflector sheet behind the LGP bounces escaped light back through the panel, increasing overall system efficiency by 15-20%.
  • Diffuser: A thin diffusion sheet in front of the LGP further homogenizes the light output and conceals the dot pattern from view.

The optical stack — LED strips, LGP, reflector, and diffuser — is precisely assembled in our controlled production environment to ensure consistent performance from unit to unit. We measure illumination uniformity on every panel and reject units that fall below our 85% uniformity threshold.

For sign shops, this engineering translates to a product you can install with confidence. No hot spots near the edges, no dark zones in the center, and no visible dot patterns through the graphic. Just clean, uniform illumination that makes your client's graphics look their best.

faqs.question: What is a light guide panel?
faqs.answer: A light guide panel (LGP) is a precision-engineered acrylic sheet that captures light injected from LEDs mounted along its edges and redirects that light forward through a pattern of extraction features on its back surface. It's the technology that enables ultra-thin light boxes with uniform illumination.

faqs.question: How thin can edge-lit light boxes actually be?
faqs.answer: Our thinnest edge-lit light boxes are 1" (25mm) deep including the frame. The LGP itself is typically 6-8mm thick. This ultra-slim profile makes edge-lit light boxes practical for applications where depth is severely constrained.

faqs.question: Are edge-lit light boxes as bright as back-lit models?
faqs.answer: Edge-lit light boxes produce slightly lower peak brightness than comparable direct-lit models, but the uniformity is often better. For most indoor applications, edge-lit brightness is more than sufficient. For high-ambient-light or outdoor applications, we recommend direct-lit systems.

faqs.question: What sizes work best with edge-lit technology?
faqs.answer: Edge-lit LGP technology is most effective for panels up to approximately 48" x 72". Beyond this size, maintaining uniformity becomes challenging and we recommend transitioning to direct-lit or multi-panel edge-lit configurations.

faqs.question: Can edge-lit light boxes be used with SEG fabric graphics?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer edge-lit SEG frames that combine LGP illumination with silicone-edge fabric graphics. This produces the slimmest possible SEG light box — as thin as 1.5" — with uniform backlit illumination through the fabric face.

title: Backlit SEG Displays | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale backlit SEG displays with tensioned fabric graphics and LED illumination. Seamless, vibrant, and changeable. German-engineered, 3-week sign trade delivery.
heroSubtitle: Tensioned fabric graphics illuminated from behind for vivid, seamless visual impact. The modern standard for premium retail, exhibit, and hospitality displays.

sections.heading: The Visual Power of Backlit Fabric Displays
sections.content:
Backlit SEG displays combine two powerful technologies — silicone edge graphics tensioning and LED backlighting — to create displays that are visually superior to virtually any other sign format. The fabric graphic stretches seamlessly across the frame with no visible edges, seams, or fasteners. The LED backlighting saturates the fabric with even illumination, producing vibrant colors and dramatic visual depth that non-lit displays simply cannot achieve.

The impact is immediate and visceral. Walk into a high-end retail store, a hotel lobby, or a trade show booth with backlit SEG displays, and the graphics command attention in a way that printed posters, vinyl banners, or even flatscreen displays cannot match. The fabric surface has a premium texture that reads as sophisticated and intentional, while the backlit illumination gives photographs and brand imagery a luminous quality that draws the eye.

Sunlite Signs produces backlit SEG displays for sign shops serving the most visually demanding commercial environments. Our German engineering ensures that every frame, LED system, and fabric graphic meets the standards required for premium installations where mediocrity is not an option.

sections.heading: System Components & Performance
sections.content:
A Sunlite backlit SEG display consists of four precisely integrated components:

  • Aluminum Extrusion Frame: Our proprietary extrusion with integrated gasket channel and LED mounting provisions. Mitered corners with precision-machined keys for rigid, true-square assembly. Available in silver, black, and white anodized finishes.
  • LED Illumination: Edge-lit or direct-lit LED systems depending on frame size and brightness requirements. Samsung modules rated at 50,000+ hours, paired with Mean Well UL-recognized power supplies. All systems carry UL 48 certification.
  • Backlit Fabric Graphic: Dye-sublimation printed on 240gsm premium backlit display fabric with medical-grade silicone gasket sewn to the perimeter. Fabric is printed at up to 1200 DPI for photographic quality.
  • Diffusion System: Precision-calibrated diffusion layer between the LEDs and the fabric to eliminate hot spots and ensure perfectly uniform illumination across the entire display surface.

The integration of these components is where Sunlite's engineering expertise shows. We don't simply put LEDs inside a frame and hope for good results. We calculate optimal LED spacing, diffusion distance, and fabric opacity for each display configuration to achieve the best possible illumination uniformity and color rendering. This systematic approach is what produces consistently excellent results across our entire product range.

sections.heading: Applications Across Industries
sections.content:
Backlit SEG displays are versatile enough for nearly any environment where visual impact matters. Here are the primary markets our sign shop partners serve with this product:

  • Retail: Feature walls, promotional displays, window backdrops, and department identification. Retail clients love the easy graphic changeability for seasonal campaigns and brand refreshes.
  • Hospitality: Hotel lobby feature walls, meeting room branding, restaurant ambiance displays, and event signage. The fabric texture and warm illumination complement hospitality interiors naturally.
  • Trade Shows & Exhibits: Portable and modular backlit displays that create dramatic booth environments. Lightweight frames pack flat for shipping, and SEG graphics fold compactly.
  • Corporate: Complementing lobby sign letters for Lobby brand walls, boardroom displays, and employee communications. Backlit SEG displays replace outdated printed posters with a modern, professional presentation.
  • Healthcare: Waiting room displays, wayfinding, and patient education graphics. The soft fabric surface and even illumination create a calming, professional atmosphere.

For multi-panel installations, our frames are designed to butt together seamlessly, allowing graphic walls of virtually any size. The frame-to-frame gap is engineered to match the visual weight of the frame perimeter, so butted panels read as a continuous display system rather than a collection of individual frames.

faqs.question: What makes backlit SEG displays better than backlit film displays?
faqs.answer: SEG fabric provides a seamless, frameless appearance that film-behind-acrylic cannot match. Fabric graphics are also easier to ship (they fold), faster to change (push into frame), and more durable (no scratching or cracking). The overall visual quality is superior.

faqs.question: Can backlit SEG graphics be washed?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our dye-sublimation printed backlit fabrics can be gently machine-washed in cold water if needed. The dye is embedded in the fiber, not printed on the surface, so it won't wash off. Air dry only — do not put in a dryer.

faqs.question: How do backlit SEG displays ship?
faqs.answer: Frames ship assembled and flat-packed for protection. Fabric graphics ship separately, folded with tissue interleaving to prevent crease marks. Any minor creasing from shipping relaxes once the graphic is tensioned in the frame.

faqs.question: Are backlit SEG displays suitable for large format walls?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Single frames can be produced up to 10' x 8'. For larger installations, multiple frames butt together seamlessly. We've supplied graphic walls up to 40 feet wide using our modular frame system.

title: Tension Fabric Displays | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale tension fabric displays with aluminum frames and dye-sub printed graphics. Backlit and non-lit options available. German-engineered, 3-week sign trade delivery.
heroSubtitle: Seamless fabric graphics stretched over precision aluminum frames. Available in backlit and non-illuminated configurations for walls, freestanding units, and hanging displays.

sections.heading: The Versatility of Tension Fabric Display Systems
sections.content:
Tension fabric displays have emerged as one of the most versatile and popular display formats in commercial signage, combining lightweight portability, premium visual quality, and effortless graphic changeability into a single product platform. The concept is straightforward: a dye-sublimation printed fabric graphic stretches over or into an aluminum frame structure, creating a taut, wrinkle-free display surface that presents graphics with clean, seamless edges.

What makes tension fabric displays so popular among sign shops is their extraordinary range of applications. The same basic technology works for illuminated wall signs, freestanding floor displays, hanging banners, trade show booths, retail fixtures, and architectural feature walls. By mastering one product platform, your shop can serve dozens of different project types with a single manufacturing partner.

Sunlite Signs produces tension fabric display systems in both SEG (silicone edge gasket) and pillow-case (slip-over) configurations, in sizes from tabletop units to full wall installations. Our German engineering ensures structural precision and finish quality that distinguishes our frames from the lightweight commodity products flooding the market from overseas factories.

sections.heading: Configurations for Every Application
sections.content:
Our wholesale tension fabric display line includes configurations for virtually every commercial display scenario:

  • Wall-Mounted Flat Frames: Single-sided displays for wall mounting. Available in backlit and non-lit versions. Slim profile from 1" to 3" depending on illumination configuration.
  • Freestanding Floor Units: Double-sided or single-sided displays with integrated base support. Available in banner-style (tall and narrow), poster-style (standard proportions), and counter-top (compact) formats.
  • Hanging Displays: Ceiling-suspended fabric displays for retail, exhibit, and event environments. Flat panel, cylinder, cube, and custom three-dimensional shapes available.
  • Modular Wall Systems: Interlocking frame components that build up into large-scale graphic walls. Individual panels can be removed for graphic changes without disassembling the entire system.

Fabric graphics for all configurations are produced using our commercial dye-sublimation printing system, which embeds the ink directly into the polyester fibers for vibrant, permanent color. Unlike surface-printed graphics that can crack, peel, or fade, dye-sublimation prints maintain their appearance through washing, handling, and years of display use.

sections.heading: Backlit vs. Non-Illuminated Fabric Displays
sections.content:
Tension fabric displays are available in both backlit and non-illuminated versions, and understanding when to specify each helps you recommend the right solution for each project:

  • Backlit Fabric Displays: Use translucent backlit display fabric with LED illumination behind. Colors appear more vivid and saturated when backlit. Ideal for feature walls, retail focal points, and any application where the display needs to command attention in its environment. More expensive due to the LED system.
  • Non-Illuminated Fabric Displays: Use opaque display fabric printed with high-density dye-sublimation. Graphics look excellent in well-lit environments using ambient or directed architectural lighting. More cost-effective than backlit, and simpler to install since no electrical connection is needed.

Many sign shops offer both options to their clients, using backlit displays for hero positions and non-lit displays for secondary locations. This tiered approach controls project costs while ensuring the most important graphics get maximum visual impact.

Sunlite Signs produces both backlit and non-lit tension fabric displays from the same frame platform, so the structural quality is identical regardless of illumination. Standard lead time is three weeks for either configuration, with fabric graphics available on a faster turnaround for replacement and seasonal change orders.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SEG and pillow-case fabric displays?
faqs.answer: SEG graphics use a silicone gasket sewn to the fabric edge that presses into a channel in the frame for a perfectly tensioned fit. Pillow-case graphics slip over the frame like a pillowcase and zip closed on the back. SEG provides tighter tension and a more premium look; pillow-case is simpler and more cost-effective.

faqs.question: Can tension fabric displays be used outdoors?
faqs.answer: Non-illuminated fabric displays can be used in covered outdoor areas. Full outdoor exposure is possible with UV-treated fabrics, though graphics will have a shorter lifespan than indoor applications. Backlit outdoor fabric displays require weather-sealed frames and are available as a custom configuration.

faqs.question: How do tension fabric displays compare to rigid panel signs?
faqs.answer: Fabric displays are lighter, easier to ship (graphics fold flat), and simpler to update. Rigid panels provide a more traditional signage look and better impact resistance. Fabric excels in environments where visual sophistication and graphic changeability are priorities.

faqs.question: Can you produce custom three-dimensional fabric display shapes?
faqs.answer: Yes. Beyond flat panels, we produce cylindrical, triangular, cubic, and custom 3D fabric display structures. These are especially popular for trade show and retail environments where a unique display shape reinforces brand identity.

faqs.question: What resolution is possible with dye-sublimation printing on fabric?
faqs.answer: Our commercial dye-sublimation system prints at up to 1200 DPI, which produces photographic-quality reproduction of images and fine typography. For best results, supply artwork at 150 DPI or higher at full output size.
